ace_test_project_guide.overview:
This guide covers everything required to create an IBM App Connect Enterprise (ACE) v13 Test Project that tests message flows using the ACE Integration Test Framework (JUnit 5 + NodeSpy API). It documents the correct project structure, required files, Java test patterns, and how to generate tests from recorded messages (.mxml files).
ACE Test Projects are the standard way to write automated unit and integration tests for ACE message flows directly inside the ACE Toolkit. They use the com.ibm.integration.test.v1 library (Integration Test Library) and JUnit 5.
From IBM documentation (https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=dit-generating-tests-from-recorded-messages-in-message-flow): "You can configure an integration server to store message assembly files in a specified directory for every message that is processed by a message flow. The test project contains a test case for every node in a message flow, apart from an input node. Each test case uses a message assembly file for input to the node and another message assembly to do a comparison check."
IBM-defined procedure (Record → Generate → Compile → [Import] → Run): 1. Configure an integration server to record messages 2. Generate a test project by using the ibmint generate tests command 3. Compile the test project and create a BAR file by using the command line 4. (Optional) Import the test project into the Toolkit for editing 5. (Optional) Run the tests using the command line or Toolkit

absolute_rule.title: DO NOT modify or mock recorded .mxml files without explicit user confirmation
absolute_rule.rule:
Recorded message files (.mxml) are immutable runtime artefacts captured by the ACE RecordedMessageManager. They represent the ground truth of what the flow actually produced at runtime. You MUST NOT:
- Edit, rewrite, or patch any .mxml file - Replace a recorded .mxml with a synthetic/hand-crafted substitute - Alter field values, checkpoint metadata, or any content inside an .mxml file - Delete or rename recorded .mxml files
unless the user has EXPLICITLY asked you to do so OR has explicitly confirmed it when you have asked them first.
Hand-crafted .mxml files (created from scratch for unrecorded paths such as error scenarios) are permitted WITHOUT confirmation because they are new files, not modifications to existing recordings. Always clearly label hand-crafted files with a distinct name (e.g. getCurrentWeather_handleError_input.mxml) so they are never confused with genuine recordings.
If you believe a recorded .mxml needs to be changed for a test to work, STOP and ask the user to confirm before touching the file. Explain why the change is needed and propose the minimal edit as a diff for review.
absolute_rule.rationale: Recorded messages are the single source of truth for what the application produced under real conditions. Silently modifying them would corrupt the regression baseline and invalidate the purpose of the test suite.
absolute_rule.permitted_without_confirmation: - COPYING recorded .mxml files from RecordedMessages directory to test resources - READING recorded .mxml files to analyse checkpoints or generate the message index - CREATING brand-new hand-crafted .mxml files for paths with no recordings
absolute_rule.requires_explicit_confirmation: - Editing any field value inside an existing recorded .mxml - Removing a checkpoint element or changing source/target metadata - Replacing a recorded .mxml with a different file - Deleting a recorded .mxml

step.title: Identify the Application under test
step.description: Note the exact application project name and confirm recorded messages exist. Naming convention: [AppName]_GeneratedTests — see 3a_test_project_structure_and_enterprise_patterns.xml.

step.title: Locate recorded messages
step.description: Recordings are stored under: [recordedMessagePath]/[AppName]/[FlowName]/[messageId]-[n].mxml If recordings exist, use them. If NOT, enable RecordedMessageManager in server.conf.yaml and invoke the flow — do NOT hand-write .mxml files (iib: namespace is complex and fragile). ibmint generate tests searches --recorded-messages recursively; pass any level (flow-level for one flow, app-level for all flows).

step.title: Generate message index to understand coverage
step.bob_instruction:
WHENEVER the user asks for the message index OR asks to analyse recorded messages, you MUST run ibmint generate msgindex — do NOT manually parse the .mxml files.
When this step is reached during test project creation, run ibmint generate msgindex against the recordings directory. This tells you exactly which paths are covered before generating tests — so you can inform the user of any coverage gaps BEFORE generating the test project.
IMPORTANT: ibmint requires the ACE profile to be loaded first. The profile sets PATH, MQSI_BASE_FILEPATH, and all required environment variables. ibmint will not be found on PATH without it.
step.why_its_useful: - Shows EXACTLY which nodes were exercised by the recordings - Directly tells you the (input .mxml, expected output .mxml) pair per node - Identifies coverage gaps (nodes with no checkpoint pair) BEFORE generating tests - Prevents incorrect checkpoint pairings that cause false test failures - The format [NodeLabel]: input; output maps 1:1 to test case structure

execution_commands.windows:
CRITICAL: On Windows, ibmint is not on PATH until mqsiprofile.cmd is sourced. PowerShell cannot source a .cmd file directly. Use cmd /c with both commands chained so the profile environment is inherited by ibmint in the same shell:
$cwd = (Get-Location).Path cmd /c "`"C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\13.0.8.0\server\bin\mqsiprofile.cmd`" && ibmint generate msgindex --recorded-messages `"$cwd\[path-to-recordings]`"" 2>&1
Key points: - Use backtick-escaped double quotes around the mqsiprofile.cmd path (has spaces) - Use && to chain: profile must succeed before ibmint runs - Wrap the recordings path in quotes if it contains spaces - ACE version in the path (13.0.8.0) may differ — check C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\ - Add 2>&1 to capture both stdout and stderr (BIP messages go to stderr)
Concrete example (WeatherAPI — top-level app recordings dir): $cwd = (Get-Location).Path cmd /c "`"C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\13.0.8.0\server\bin\mqsiprofile.cmd`" && ibmint generate msgindex --recorded-messages `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_RecordedMessages_Orig\WeatherAPI_App`"" 2>&1
execution_commands.linux_aix:
Source the profile then run ibmint in the same shell session:
source /opt/ibm/ace/13.0.8.0/server/bin/mqsiprofile ibmint generate msgindex --recorded-messages /path/to/recordings/AppName/

step.reading_the_output:
Each output block covers one MessageID (one recorded invocation). Within each block, each line maps directly to one test case: NodeLabel: input.mxml; output.mxml
A node with no input file (e.g. "HTTP Input: ; -0.mxml") is an input-type node — it triggers the flow but is not directly testable via NodeSpy.
A node with no output file (e.g. "HTTP Reply: -2.mxml;") is a terminal node — messages arrive here but it does not propagate further.
Example ibmint output: MessageID: 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001 HTTP Input: ; 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001-0.mxml Prepare Request: 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001-0.mxml; 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001-1.mxml HTTP Request: 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001-1.mxml; 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001-2.mxml HTTP Reply: 000071A0-6A4EAD36-00000001-2.mxml;
Means: Test "Prepare Request": input=-0.mxml expected=-1.mxml terminal=out Test "HTTP Request": input=-1.mxml expected=-2.mxml terminal=out (live call — use ignorePath)
A session missing a node means that path was NOT exercised in that invocation. Example — missing "HTTP Request" line means failure terminal fired (400 path): MessageID: 00008E3C-6A4EB973-00000002 HTTP Input: ; 00008E3C-6A4EB973-00000002-0.mxml Prepare Request: 00008E3C-6A4EB973-00000002-0.mxml; 00008E3C-6A4EB973-00000002-1.mxml HTTP Reply: 00008E3C-6A4EB973-00000002-1.mxml; → "Prepare Request" propagated via failure terminal (no HTTP Request in this session)
step.coverage_gap_detection:
After running the index, scan ALL sessions for nodes that NEVER appear as a source. Any node defined in the msgflow that never appears as a source in ANY session has zero recorded output — it was never exercised.
The correct fix for a coverage gap is to RECORD MORE MESSAGES: 1. Configure the scenario that exercises the missing path (e.g. use an invalid API key to trigger a backend failure, triggering the Handle Error path). 2. Invoke the flow with that scenario while recording is enabled. 3. Use the newly captured .mxml files when generating the test project.
Do NOT hand-write .mxml files to fill coverage gaps. Hand-crafted files are inadvisable — the iib: namespace schema is complex, values must exactly match what ACE would produce at runtime, and the files are fragile to maintain.

step.title: Generate the test project using ibmint generate tests (PRIMARY PATH)
step.bob_instruction:
ALWAYS use ibmint generate tests as the FIRST approach to creating a test project. Do NOT manually create the project structure, .project file, build.gradle, or Java test class from scratch. ibmint generate tests scaffolds all of this automatically from the recorded messages — and the resulting project is guaranteed to be structurally correct and importable into the ACE Toolkit.
Manual project creation (old Steps 5+6) is the FALLBACK only when: - ibmint is not available (ACE version below 12.0.8) - No recorded messages exist at all
After generating, the workflow continues at Step 5 (review and enhance).
step.description:
Run ibmint generate tests against a recordings directory. ibmint will: - Recursively search all subdirectories of --recorded-messages for .mxml files - Create a complete Gradle-based test project structure - Generate a Java test class with one test method per node transition per session - Copy all .mxml files into src/main/resources/ - Wire up testproject.descriptor referencing the application under test
PREREQUISITE: Gradle 8.2.1 or higher must be installed separately. ibmint generate tests generates a Gradle build (build.gradle), NOT a Maven pom.xml. Install Gradle from https://gradle.org/install/ before running this command.
All three parameters (--recorded-messages, --output-test-project, --java-class) are REQUIRED. There are no optional parameters except --help.
--recorded-messages is recursive: you can pass any directory level (app-level, flow-level, or any parent). ibmint walks all subdirectories automatically. Passing a flow-level dir generates tests only for that flow. Passing an app-level dir generates tests for all flows in that app.

execution_commands.windows:
Run via cmd /c so the ACE profile is active in the same shell:
$cwd = (Get-Location).Path cmd /c "`"C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\13.0.8.0\server\bin\mqsiprofile.cmd`" && ibmint generate tests --recorded-messages `"$cwd\[recordingsDir]`" --output-test-project `"$cwd\[AppName]_GeneratedTests`" --java-class com.[org].[app].test.[ClassName]" 2>&1
Concrete example (WeatherAPI — all flows, app-level dir): $cwd = (Get-Location).Path cmd /c "`"C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\13.0.8.0\server\bin\mqsiprofile.cmd`" && ibmint generate tests --recorded-messages `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_RecordedMessages_Orig\WeatherAPI_App`" --output-test-project `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_GeneratedTests`" --java-class com.weather.api.test.WeatherAPITest" 2>&1
Key points: - --recorded-messages is searched recursively — pass any level that contains .mxml files - --output-test-project is where the new Gradle project will be created - ACE version in the profile path may differ — check C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\ - Add 2>&1 to capture both stdout and stderr (BIP messages go to stderr) - Gradle must be installed separately (not bundled with ACE)

step.what_is_generated: The generated Java class contains: - One @Test method per node transition per recorded session - NodeSpy.evaluate() calls with the correct -0, -1, -2 checkpoint files - equalsMessage() assertions for each transition - @AfterEach TestSetup.restoreAllMocks() cleanup
step.after_generation: After ibmint generate tests completes: 1. Review the generated Java class — it is a starting point, not a final test suite 2. Proceed to Step 5 to enhance the generated tests 3. Import into the ACE Toolkit: File → Import → Existing Projects into Workspace (the Toolkit auto-detects it as a Test Project from testproject.descriptor)

step.title: Review and enhance the generated tests
step.description: ibmint generate tests produces a mechanically correct but intentionally minimal test suite. The generated tests verify exact message equality for every node transition. Before using the test project for regression testing, review and enhance:

enhancements.enhancement: Add ignorePath() calls on HTTP Request tests for all time-varying fields. Generated tests will fail on the next run if the response body, Date header, CDN headers, or rate-limit counters change. See common_failures/timestamp_mismatch.
Rename test methods from generated identifiers (e.g. test_0_PrepareRequest_out_0) to descriptive names that explain the scenario under test.
Add comments explaining which PATH (A/B/C) each test exercises, and which session ID the recordings came from. Makes maintenance far easier.

For unrecorded paths (coverage gaps identified in Step 3), record additional messages rather than writing hand-crafted .mxml files. Re-run ibmint generate tests after recording to include the new paths automatically.
Group related test methods into @Nested inner classes (one per flow or one per path) to improve test output readability in CI/CD pipelines.

row.assertion: equalsMessage(expected) — exact match against recorded output
equalsMessage with ignorePath() for time-varying fields, OR setStopAtInputTerminal for offline

row.test_type: Not directly testable via NodeSpy (it IS the trigger)
row.assertion: Use the HTTP Input checkpoint (-0.mxml) as INPUT to the first Compute node test

row.test_type: Not directly testable via NodeSpy (terminal node)
row.assertion: Verify upstream node propagates to correct terminal

step.title: FALLBACK ONLY: Manually create the test project structure
step.description: Use this step ONLY when ibmint generate tests is unavailable (ACE below v12.0.8) or when no recorded messages exist. If you used Step 4 successfully, skip this step. Use the file templates in 3a_test_project_structure_and_enterprise_patterns.xml to create all required files, then write the Java test class using patterns in 5b_java_test_patterns.xml.

mistake.problem: Using wrong .project natures — "Unknown referenced nature" warning
mistake.symptom: ACE Toolkit shows: "Unknown referenced nature: org.eclipse.buildship.core.gradleprojectnature" This happens because the Buildship (Eclipse Gradle) plugin is not installed in the ACE Toolkit.
mistake.solution: Use ALL four ACE Toolkit natures and the full builder chain from the .project template in 3a_test_project_structure_and_enterprise_patterns.xml. Do NOT include org.eclipse.buildship.core.gradleprojectnature — the build.gradle file can still exist for command-line Gradle builds without it.

mistake.symptom: TestException at runtime: node not found
mistake.solution: The label in .node("...") must exactly match the translation string in the .msgflow (the string="..." attribute). Example: string="Prepare Request" → .node("Prepare Request")

ibmint_test_commands.overview: IBM App Connect Enterprise v12.0.8+ provides ibmint CLI commands to automate test generation and analysis from recorded messages. These commands run from the IBM App Connect Enterprise Console (not the Toolkit) and require mqsiprofile to be sourced first.

command.name: ibmint generate msgindex
command.description: Analyses recorded messages in a directory and prints a message index showing which nodes were exercised by each .mxml file and the propagation path. Use this BEFORE generating tests to understand coverage and map .mxml → node.
command.examples:
# Analyse recordings for an application (Linux/AIX) ibmint generate msgindex --recorded-messages recordings/AppName/
# Analyse recordings on Windows ibmint generate msgindex --recorded-messages C:\temp\RecordedMessages\AppName\
command.sample_output:
BIP15215I: Found '14' recorded messages in directory 'C:\temp\RecordedMessages\AppName\'. BIP15220I: Generating index from recorded messages.
MessageID: 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001
MQ.APP.INPUT: ; 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-0.mxml Flow Order: 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-0.mxml; 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-1.mxml, ... Store Input Message: 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-1.mxml; 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-2.mxml Load Properties: 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-2.mxml; 00006977-68DD7AF7-00000001-3.mxml ...
command.how_to_read: Each line shows: NodeName: InputCheckpoint; OutputCheckpoint(s) The format is: [NodeLabel]: [input .mxml]; [output .mxml(s)] This tells you exactly which checkpoint pair to use for each node's test case.

command.name: ibmint generate tests
command.description:
PRIMARY method for creating ACE test projects. Automatically generates a complete JUnit 5 Gradle test project from recorded messages. Available in ACE v12.0.8 and above. The generated project is structurally correct and importable into the ACE Toolkit without any manual file creation.
ALWAYS prefer this command over manual project creation. See workflow Step 4.
Source: https://www.ibm.com/docs/en/app-connect/13.0.x?topic=commands-ibmint-generate-tests-command

parameters.parameter: Fully qualified (or relative) path to a directory containing recorded .mxml files. The command searches ALL subdirectories recursively, so you can pass any level: - A single flow dir: recordings/WeatherAPI_App/getCurrentWeather (tests one flow) - An app-level dir: recordings/WeatherAPI_App (tests all flows) Be careful not to pass a very broad path (e.g. /) — it will try to process everything.
Name or path of the directory where the generated test project will be created. Use the [AppName]_GeneratedTests naming convention.
Fully-qualified Java class name for the generated test class. Example: com.weather.api.test.WeatherAPITest
Output command help information to the console.

prerequisites.prerequisite: Gradle version 8.2.1 or higher must be installed BEFORE running this command. Gradle is NOT bundled with ACE — install from https://gradle.org/install/ The generated project is Gradle-based (build.gradle), NOT Maven (pom.xml).

command.examples:
# Windows — WeatherAPI app-level dir (cmd /c to inherit ACE profile) $cwd = (Get-Location).Path cmd /c "`"C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\13.0.8.0\server\bin\mqsiprofile.cmd`" && ibmint generate tests --recorded-messages `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_RecordedMessages_Orig\WeatherAPI_App`" --output-test-project `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_GeneratedTests`" --java-class com.weather.api.test.WeatherAPITest" 2>&1
# Windows — single flow dir only cmd /c "`"C:\Program Files\IBM\ACE\13.0.8.0\server\bin\mqsiprofile.cmd`" && ibmint generate tests --recorded-messages `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_RecordedMessages_Orig\WeatherAPI_App\getCurrentWeather`" --output-test-project `"$cwd\WeatherAPI_App_GeneratedTests`" --java-class com.weather.api.test.WeatherAPITest" 2>&1
# Linux / AIX — absolute paths (from IBM docs example) ibmint generate tests \ --recorded-messages ~/Dev/recordings/AppName \ --output-test-project ~/Dev/AppName_GeneratedTests \ --java-class com.org.app.test.AppTest
# Linux / AIX — relative paths ibmint generate tests \ --recorded-messages AppName \ --output-test-project ../AppName_GeneratedTests \ --java-class com.org.app.test.AppTest

notes.note: AIX: recorded messages must be copied (via scp) to a Windows/Linux development environment with ACE Toolkit and Console before running this command.

After generation: File → Import → Existing Projects into Workspace in the ACE Toolkit (auto-detected as a Test Project from testproject.descriptor). Then proceed to workflow Step 5 to review and enhance the generated tests.

command.name: ibmint deploy (test project)
command.description: Deploys a test project BAR file to an integration node and server. The application under test must already be deployed to the same server.

command.name: IntegrationServer --test-project
command.description: Runs all tests in a deployed test project on a standalone integration server. This is the command used to execute tests in CI/CD pipelines and automation. The server MUST have start-msgflows set to false so only the test framework runs.

parameters.parameter: --no-nodejs: Disable Node.js runtime (not needed for test execution)
--start-msgflows false: Do not start the application flows — only run tests
--mq-queue-manager-name: Required only if the flows use MQ
--test-project: Name of the test project to run (must match deployed project name)

command.examples:
# Run tests on AIX (MQ application example) IntegrationServer \ --work-dir /var/mqsi/components/<BrokerName>/servers/<ServerName> \ --no-nodejs \ --start-msgflows false \ --mq-queue-manager-name <QueueManagerName> \ --test-project AppName_GeneratedTests
# Run tests on Windows (WeatherAPI example — no MQ) IntegrationServer \ --work-dir ace_demo \ --no-nodejs \ --start-msgflows false \ --test-project WeatherAPI_App_GeneratedTests

important_notes.note: Tests run at the Integration SERVER level, NOT the Integration NODE level. Configurations in node.conf.yaml are NOT applied. Add any required settings (truststores, keystores, etc.) to the server's overrides/server.conf.yaml instead.
Review test results in the console output. Non-zero exit code indicates test failures.
